crowdfunding donations can't immediately withdrawn recipient, they pass this test, Shenkman said.
"You have this absurd result, that people giving small dollar amounts actually have gift-tax reporting requirement that they violate," said Shenkman.
Another headache donors: their contributions aren't deductible unless they donate qualified organization such 501(c)(3) nonprofit.

platform also allows recipients that list describe their campaigns "charity" website.
website Generosity, platform "socially conscious fundraising" owned Indiegogo, states that donors should contact campaign organizers determine donations deductible.
website advertises campaigns that claim benefit registered nonprofits alongside campaigns that make such claims.
Donations even create unintended headaches recipients.
beneficiary receives means-tested government benefits such Medicaid Supplemental Security Income, then receiving large cash inflow from crowdfunding campaign could cause lose government benefits, said Jonathan Blattmachr, principal Pioneer Wealth Partners York director estate planning Peak Trust Company.
That could mean that, example, funds from crowdfunding medical expenses that Medicaid would otherwise have covered.
"You might think you're helping recipient, you're actually hurting her," Blattmachr said.
Raising funds behalf supplemental special needs trust, individuals seeking help with medical bills, would circumvent problem, Shenkman said.
Perhaps biggest problem donors, however, difficulty evaluating whether funds will actually used purposes described crowdfunding campaign's pitch.
GoFundMe, Indiegogo's Generosity YouCaring have procedures place guard against misuse their platforms, according spokespersons each companies.
it's impossible crowdfunding companies thoroughly every organizer recipient uses their platforms, said Daniel Borochoff, founder CharityWatch.
"It's convenient, it's powerful because reach people quickly, whether money actually goes intended purpose certainly guaranteed," Borochoff said.
Borochoff said donors should only contribute crowdfunding campaign they trust organizer, such someone they personally know respected community leader.
donors want contribute non-profit organization, they should directly through organization itself rather than through crowdfunding website, which collect fees from donations, said.
contrast crowdfunding campaigns, Borochoff said, tax-exempt nonprofits subjected oversight board directors regulators, must submit annual filings Internal Revenue Service.
Traditional nonprofits also have more experience vetting potential recipients, said.
"With these websites it's very easy feel good donating money," said Borochoff.
"But whether you're doing good unclear."
